item.title: Govt admits there is no excuse for 6-year delay in rebuilding Tailevu North College classroom block
item.description: Govt admits there is no excuse for 6-year delay in rebuilding Tailevu North College classroom block

item.title: WATCH: Fijian Media Association Korovou Town Hall Forum
item.description: The Fijian Media Association Town Hall Forum was held at the Tailevu Hotel, where the people of Tailevu were given the opportunity to directly question Cabinet ministers, Opposition leaders and senior government officials on issues that matter most to their communities, from roads, water and health services to agriculture, market access and rural development.
item.content:encoded: The Fijian Media Association Town Hall Forum was held at the Tailevu Hotel, where the people of Tailevu were given the opportunity to directly question Cabinet ministers, Opposition leaders and senior government officials on issues that matter most to their communities, from roads, water and health services to agriculture, market access and rural development.

item.title: Customary fishing rights owners to receive 80% of royalties under new regulations
item.description: Owners of customary fishing rights will now receive 80 percent of royalties collected when certain natural resources are extracted from State land in areas linked to their customary fishing rights. A People’s Alliance Party statement says under new regulations introduced by Minister for Lands and Mineral Resources Filimoni Vosarogo, the remaining 20 percent of the royalties will go to the State.
item.content:encoded:
Owners of customary fishing rights will now receive 80 percent of royalties collected when certain natural resources are extracted from State land in areas linked to their customary fishing rights.

A People’s Alliance Party statement says under new regulations introduced by Minister for Lands and Mineral Resources Filimoni Vosarogo, the remaining 20 percent of the royalties will go to the State.

This means that when resources such as sand, gravel, clay, soil, rock, top soil and river spalls are extracted, the owners of customary fishing rights will receive the larger share of the royalty payments.

The new royalty rate for sand and gravel is $10.40 per cubic metre, while clay and soil are set at $6.50 per cubic metre.

The rate for rock is $5.21 per cubic metre, top soil is $38.99 per cubic metre and river spalls are $7.80 per cubic metre.

Sand extracted from drainage and irrigation dump sites will attract a royalty rate of $8 per cubic metre.

The regulations also require these royalty rates to be reviewed every five years to ensure they remain fair and reflect market conditions.

The People’s Alliance Party says the move is aimed at ensuring iTaukei resource owners receive a fairer share of the benefits from Fiji’s natural resources.

It says Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka and the party believe that people who have protected these resources for generations should receive a fair return from their use.

The party says the new regulations will help strengthen communities and ensure development provides direct benefits to resource owners.

item.title: Abolish COC - Nata
item.description: The Constitutional Offices Commission (COC) should be abolished and its responsibilities transferred to a parliamentary joint committee, according to former journalist and publisher Josefa Nata. Speaking before the Constitution Review Commission at Suvavou House, Nata says the commission's functions should instead be carried out by a joint committee of Parliament's two Houses, which can also be em
item.content:encoded:
The Constitutional Offices Commission (COC) should be abolished and its responsibilities transferred to a parliamentary joint committee, according to former journalist and publisher Josefa Nata.

Speaking before the Constitution Review Commission at Suvavou House, Nata says the commission's functions should instead be carried out by a joint committee of Parliament's two Houses, which can also be empowered to conduct special investigations and recommend prosecutions.

Nata also proposed that Cabinet ministers be appointed from outside Parliament, drawing from professionals, industry leaders and other accomplished individuals rather than elected MPs.

He says appointing people who have already established themselves professionally and financially could help minimise corruption at the highest levels of government.

Nata further recommend that the Constitution place a limit on the size of Cabinet to prevent what he described as "outrageously bloated" governments formed for political expediency.

He also proposed the creation of a Secretary for Nation Building to oversee national reconciliation, ethnic harmony and efforts to unite the country.

During his submission, Nata also welcomed the recent recommendation by the Republic of Fiji Military Forces Commander, Major General Ro Jone Kalouniwai, to remove constitutional immunity provisions.

He says the Commander's position should be given serious consideration, describing it as an important contribution to the national conversation on constitutional immunity.

Nata says immunity provisions should "never ever be entertained" and welcomed the military's support for their removal as a step towards ending what he described as a culture of impunity.

He also suggested that the Supreme Court may have been reluctant to address the immunity provisions because of concerns about antagonising the military and creating further instability, but says the Commander's submission had changed that conversation.

Nata praised Major General Kalouniwai's leadership following the 2022 General Election, saying the nation owed him "a deep debt of gratitude".

He further called for the removal of statutory time limits for the offence of treason, saying there should be no limitation period for prosecuting those accused of attempting to overthrow the State.

item.title: Three people arrested following Cunningham robbery
item.description: Priya Nand , Antornette Vuniuca · 04/07/2026 Three people have been arrested in connection with a robbery case in Cunningham yesterday morning.
item.content:encoded:
Priya Nand , Antornette Vuniuca · 04/07/2026

Three people have been arrested in connection with a robbery case in Cunningham yesterday morning.

Police say the incident was captured on CCTV.

They say the suspect initially alleged to be involved in the robbery was arrested by Valelevu Police, while investigations into the initial report led to information about the alleged involvement of two other people, who were also arrested.

Police say the three suspects are also alleged to be linked to other robbery cases in the area.

They say the suspects remain in custody at the Valelevu Police Station as investigations continue.

Cunningham Stage 1 resident Sheetal Chand had told fijivillage News that she was robbed in her driveway while leaving for work early in the morning.

Chand said the incident happened at about 6.30am when a man opened the rear door of her vehicle, grabbed her handbag and lunch bag, and ran off as she was reversing out of her driveway.

item.title: Rewa women call for better market access and business support
item.description: Women in Rewa are calling for better access to markets, equipment to support their income-generating activities and business management training to help grow and expand their enterprises. The concerns were raised by members of the Vunuku Village Women's Group during a talanoa session with the Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection, Sashi Kiran following the closing of the Mud Crab Fatt
item.content:encoded:
Women in Rewa are calling for better access to markets, equipment to support their income-generating activities and business management training to help grow and expand their enterprises.

The concerns were raised by members of the Vunuku Village Women's Group during a talanoa session with the Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection, Sashi Kiran following the closing of the Mud Crab Fattening Training in Tavuya Villag e.

The women showcased their handmade mats, screen-printed products, jewellery, mud crabs and shrimps while discussing the challenges they face in accessing markets.

Responding to the issues raised, Kiran outlined the range of assistance available through the Ministry of Women and encouraged the group to explore new income-generating opportunities by making the most of Rewa's rich natural and cultural assets.

She highlighted the growing demand for authentic Fijian souvenirs, traditional Rewan cuisine, eco-tourism experiences and village-based tourism, saying these present exciting opportunities for women to generate sustainable incomes while preserving their culture and heritage.

Kiran says through continued collaboration with communities and partner agencies, the Ministry will support women's groups to strengthen their enterprises, access new economic opportunities and build sustainable livelihoods that benefit their families and contribute to the growth of rural communities.

Accompanied by Assistant Roko Tui Rewa Vereniki Vulibulu, Kiran visited the women's group after stopping at the Tavuya Village Kindergarten, where she met with early childhood education students and their teacher, acknowledging the important role early learning plays in giving children the best start in life.

item.title: 7 Miles crash causes congestion through Nakasi and Laqere
item.description: A two-vehicle collision at 7 Miles this morning is causing heavy traffic congestion for drivers travelling through Nakasi, Laqere and surrounding areas. The Fiji Police Force is yet to confirm details of the crash, including whether there were any injuries or fatalities.
item.content:encoded:
A two-vehicle collision at 7 Miles this morning is causing heavy traffic congestion for drivers travelling through Nakasi, Laqere and surrounding areas.

The Fiji Police Force is yet to confirm details of the crash, including whether there were any injuries or fatalities.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Sawani resident Rohit Sharma, who was caught in the traffic, says the collision involved two vehicles and the damage appeared to be extensive.

Sharma says he had left home at around 6.30am and made a stop in Nadawa before continuing his journey.

He said that by 7.48am, he was still stuck in traffic as police and other authorities attended the scene.

Drivers are advised to expect delays and exercise caution while travelling through the area.

item.title: Work begins on Fiji's largest solar farm at Nadi International Airport
item.description: Construction has officially begun on the Nadi International Airport Solar Farm, which is expected to become the largest solar farm in Fiji. Speaking at the groundbreaking ceremony, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Tourism and Civil Aviation Viliame Gavoka says the 3.25-megawatt project will help strengthen Fiji's renewable energy efforts while improving energy security.
item.content:encoded:
Construction has officially begun on the Nadi International Airport Solar Farm, which is expected to become the largest solar farm in Fiji.

Speaking at the groundbreaking ceremony, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Tourism and Civil Aviation Viliame Gavoka says the 3.25-megawatt project will help strengthen Fiji's renewable energy efforts while improving energy security.

Gavoka says the first phase of the project will power airport operations with renewable energy, reduce reliance on traditional energy sources, lower operating costs and reduce Fiji Airports' carbon footprint.

He says Nadi International Airport is Fiji's main gateway to the world, and it is important that it has reliable and modern infrastructure.

Gavoka says the project supports Fiji's long-term commitment to renewable energy and sustainable development.

He also highlighted Fiji Airports' recent achievements, including receiving the 2026 Green Airports Recognition Platinum Award and being named the Best Airport in the South Pacific earlier this year.

Gavoka says the solar farm has been designed to meet aviation safety standards, and thanked Fiji Airports and its project partners for helping deliver the development.

item.title: Commission hears calls for greater powers for Permanent Secretaries and mandatory investment of resource income in USP submission
item.description: Greater decision-making powers should be returned to Permanent Secretaries instead of being concentrated in the hands of Ministers
